title: oreocnide frutescens subsp. frutescens
intro: Oreocnide frutescens subsp. frutescens, also known as Chinese snake root, is a species of flowering plant in the nettle family that is native to China and Southeast Asia. It is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of compounds such as glycosides and phenolic acids. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain and inflammation, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Oreocnide frutescens subsp. frutescens can be prepared for medicinal use through infusion, decoction, or tincture, allowing for its bioactive compounds to be extracted and utilized for therapeutic purposes.
